title: Add coordinator retry for swarm control actions
goal: Implement a bounded issue #13 UX/reliability slice so assign_task, assign_next, and task_control style swarm tool actions reactively self-promote on coordinator-denial errors, matching the existing spawn retry flow and reducing manual recovery during orchestration.
requiredInputs: Coordinator-only server responses for assign_task, assign_next, and task_control
permissions: Modify swarm communicate tool coordinator retry helpers and focused tests
Run cargo fmt/check/test and selfdev build
completionConditions: Coordinator-only swarm tool actions for assignment/control detect coordinator denial and self-promote the current session before retrying once.
The existing spawn self-promotion behavior and error copy remain compatible.
Focused tests cover generic coordinator denial detection and actionable retry copy for assign/control retries.
cargo fmt/check/test, git diff --check, selfdev build/reload, governance, commit, and push pass.
failureTaxonomy: Retrying non-coordinator errors or masking real request failures
Infinite retry loops after self-promotion fails
Changing server authorization semantics instead of tool-side retry UX
Breaking existing spawn coordinator retry behavior
notes: Keep this tool-side only. Retry at most once after CommAssignRole current->coordinator succeeds. Do not change server permission rules.
